quotes.text: complexity very, very bad. say again: complexity very bad. you not smart enough to manage it, nobody is.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug very impressed with type systems when young grug. type systems shiny and wonderful. then grug mass mass if/else on union type and mass mass casting everywhere and grug not mass impressed anymore.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: grug no mass mass like closure. closure make code look mass smart but grug mass mass debug closure and mass mass not fun.
quotes.author: The Grug Brained Developer

quotes.text: Simple is not easy. Easy is near to our capabilities. Simple is about lack of interleaving. They are completely orthogonal.
quotes.author: Rich Hickey

quotes.text: Some people, when confronted with a problem, think 'I know, I'll use regular expressions.' Now they have two problems.
quotes.author: Jamie Zawinski

quotes.text: Every good abstraction starts as 'this is so simple!' and ends as 'I can't believe how complex this is.'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If you need a PhD to understand your deployment pipeline, you've built a research project, not a product.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every SaaS goes through the same phases: 'We'll keep it simple' → 'We need an enterprise tier' → 'What is a microservice?'
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every 'easy' feature request is just code someone else doesn't have to write.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The user who says 'it's just a simple feature' has never written a line of code in their life.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your product has a 'getting started' guide that's 47 pages long, your product is too complicated.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your CSS file is over 500 lines, you're either doing something wrong or you're building Bootstrap.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your feature flags are more complex than your code, you've built a configuration management system.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every feature you add creates a new path for users to get confused. Add features like you'd add rooms to a house: with purpose.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: If your database has more than 50 tables, you probably need a domain expert, not a better ORM.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best SaaS demos are 5 minutes long. If yours is 30 minutes, your product is too complex.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The purpose of software engineering is to control complexity, not to create it.
quotes.author: Barbara Liskov

quotes.text: Simplicity does not precede complexity, but follows it.
quotes.author: Alan Perlis

quotes.text: Controlling complexity is the essence of computer programming.
quotes.author: Brian Kernighan

quotes.text: Your product's complexity is your enemy. Simplify relentlessly.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Maybe I’m taking crazy pills, but I’m still stuck on “why the hell does a TUI need to run in terminal React by way of JavaScript” The fact that Anthropic felt the need to buy a runtime so they could make their TUI better speaks more to the quality of engineering than anything else IMO. If rewrites are so easy, why not rewrite CC in a native language? Would’ve been a hell of a lot cheaper.
quotes.author: weakfish
quotes.source: Hacker News — Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
quotes.extraction_note: Humorous critique of unnecessary complexity in software toolchains, with a grug-brained call for simplicity.
